pattern: Pairing the sorted seat positions with the sorted student positions in order always minimizes the total distance moved, so sorting both sides is the entire algorithm.
hints: The order in which students pick seats doesn't matter for the total distance if you're free to choose it — only which seat ends up with which student.
Sorting both arrays and pairing them up index by index never does worse than any other pairing, because any crossing assignment can be uncrossed without increasing total distance.
Sort seats and students independently, then sum abs(seats[i] - students[i]) for every i.

edge_inputs.description: seats and students already coincide, needs zero moves
seats and students in reversed order
extreme constraint values at both ends
all seats and students at the same position

stress.description: random seat and student positions across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    seats = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    students = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [seats, students]
